## [00:00 - 03:02]

This is the pubcast shot. I'm John Lennon. I know him. Let's go. If you're gonna run Facebook ads, know the rules. Otherwise, honestly, just expect to get your ads rejected. And if you do it often, expect to get disabled ad accounts, disabled pages, business managers. Of course, we don't want that. So first up, you need to read Facebook's ad policies. And some of it is just common sense, right? Don't break the law. Don't line deceive. Don't discriminate. Don't swear. Don't promote dangerous products. And really, if you're ethical, a lot of this stuff is going to be easy. But I realize that some of these rules are less obvious. There are some industries that are allowed, but they're restricted. For example, special ad categories limit how you target and run ads. So politics, elections, housing, credit, employment, when you create a campaign promoting any of those things, select the special ad category. Then the targeting is going to be restricted. Don't try to get around all that. But other things come up a lot too, like no personal attributes. You can't suggest that you know something about somebody like race, religion, age, sexual orientation, gender, disability, financial status, etc. So like you can promote financial services that help people manage debt, but you can't suggest that the targeted user is in financial trouble. Or you can promote a medication and mention its benefits, but you can't suggest that medication would help their condition. Also avoid non functional landing pages. So your ad might be fine, but it directs to a problematic landing page, error pages, automatic downloads, content that doesn't follow through on whatever it was you promised in your ad, inability to navigate away from that landing page. Also avoid non existing functionality. So images with buttons or checkboxes that don't do anything. So an example would be a thumbnail image on a video with a play button. Avoid health issues. So like no before and after photos, for example, don't show unnecessary skin. Facebook doesn't like that. MLM, multi level marketing, avoid that stuff. Avoid misleading claims, unrealistic guarantees, get rich quick schemes, make X dollars in 30 days. Also watch out with Facebook brand assets. They're really sensitive about that. Avoid using Facebook logos if you can. Oh, screenshots of the Facebook experience must represent the current UI. I've messed up on that one. So sometimes ads are rejected by mistake. And there are automatic filters that review your ads first, and you might need an appeal for a human review. And understand

## [03:02 - 04:18]

that everyone has ads rejected occasionally if you run enough ads. It is difficult to avoid, especially those incorrect rejections. But do all you can to avoid it. Know the rules. Follow them.
